meta.title: Tabs
meta.description: Displays a section with ThemeHeading and vertical tab navigation on the left with switchable content panels on the right. On mobile, tabs become accordions.

parentTsx: useBlockProps, useInnerBlocksProps, store as blockEditorStore from @wordpress/block-editor
childTsx: useBlockProps, useInnerBlocksProps, InspectorControls, BlockControls, store as blockEditorStore from @wordpress/block-editor
PanelBody, TextControl, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarButton, Popover from @wordpress/components

requiredPatterns.usage: Show/hide content based on whether any fields are populated or inner blocks exist
Sync block name in list view with heading content
React context to pass active tab index from parent to children
useEffect to sync child tabTitle attributes to parent tabsHeading array
Custom appender for adding new tab items
Add settings button to child block toolbar

componentStructure.tabsArea: div (contains left tab buttons and right content panel)
componentStructure.tabButtonsList: div.tabs-buttons (vertical list of tab buttons on desktop)

qualityChecklist: Content panel on right (desktop) with accent-lighter background
Accent color on active tab button and content panel
One tab always selected on desktop
Multiple or no tabs open on mobile
When switching mobile to desktop: if all closed, first tab selected
tabsHeading synced from child tabTitle values
TabsCurrent React context for editor tab visibility
Frontend JS handles tab switching, keyboard nav, ARIA
Plus/minus toggle icon on mobile accordion items

parent.name: eyebrow
parent.description: Small uppercase text above the heading

parent.name: heading
parent.description: Main heading text (Anton font). Used for renameBlock().

parent.name: buttons
parent.description: CTA buttons in the header area

parent.name: tabsHeading
parent.description: Array of tab titles synced from child tabTitle attributes. Auto-managed, do not edit directly.

child.name: anchor
child.description: HTML anchor/ID for the tab item

child.name: tabTitle
child.description: Tab button label text. Synced to parent tabsHeading array.

child.name: tabDescription
child.description: Short description text that appears below the tab title on the active tab button (both desktop and mobile). Visible in the tab button itself, not in the content panel.

desktopLayout.description: Two-column layout below the ThemeHeading. Left column contains vertical tab buttons. Right column shows the content panel for the active tab.
desktopLayout.sectionPadding: py-10 sm:py-16 (64px top/bottom on desktop, matching Accordion pattern)
desktopLayout.containerGap: gap-16 (64px between heading and tabs area)

tabsArea.gap: none (tab buttons touch the content panel edge)

tabButtonsColumn.verticalPadding: py-16 (64px top/bottom for the button list)

desktop.description: Rounded-left pill shape with left border only (border-l, border-t, border-b, no border-r). Contains heading text and optional description.

inactive.border: border border-charcoal (but only left, top, bottom - no right border)
inactive.borderRadius: rounded-l-3xl (25px left corners only, no right corners)

active.height: auto (expands to fit content including description)

mobile.description: Full-width rounded box (accordion style). Has a plus/minus icon for open/close toggle.

inactive.layout: flex items-start gap-4 (title left, icon right)

active.contentGap: gap-4 (16px between header row and content)

desktop.description: Right-side panel that displays the active tab's inner block content. Has a lighter accent background and rounded corners.
desktop.background: bg-accent-lighter (lighter shade of page accent color)
desktop.headingFont: font-heading text-header-5 (Anton, 40px) - this is the repeated tab title shown at the top of the panel
desktop.innerContentLayout: Inner blocks rendered as prose content. Sub-sections separated by horizontal dividers (border-t border-charcoal).
desktop.learnMoreButtons: Tertiary-style link buttons with right arrow: 'LEARN MORE ->' uppercase

mobile.description: On mobile, the content panel is NOT a separate element. Content appears inline inside the open accordion item, below the title and description.
mobile.background: Same accent color as the tab button (bg-accent, NOT bg-accent-lighter)
mobile.padding: Inherits from the accordion item padding
mobile.note: The 'second title' (the one that appears at the top of the desktop content panel) is HIDDEN on mobile per Figma annotation. Only the tab title in the accordion header is visible.

figmaAnnotations.annotation: Click for Select state. Colour will change based on the pillar. Description appears for active tab. One tab always active on default.
Second title (Tab title) is hidden on mobile, only main (this one) is visible
Ability to stylize text: bold, italic, bullet points, numbered lists, etc.

props.note: Two-column layout: left = eyebrow+heading, right = description+buttons. headingSize=3 maps to text-header-3 (H3 style = font/size/5xl = 56px desktop per Figma)

tabsAreaDesktop.description: Two-column area: tab buttons left, content panel right. Hidden on mobile.

tabButtonsListDesktop.description: Vertical list of tab buttons on the left side

tabButtonDesktopInactive.classes: tabs-button border-l border-t border-b border-charcoal rounded-l-3xl p-6 h-[140px] flex flex-col gap-6 items-start justify-center w-full text-left transition-colors cursor-pointer
tabButtonDesktopInactive.description: Inactive tab button with left-only border and rounded left corners

tabButtonDesktopActive.classes: tabs-button selected border-l border-t border-b border-charcoal rounded-l-3xl p-6 flex flex-col gap-6 items-start justify-center w-full text-left bg-accent transition-colors cursor-pointer
tabButtonDesktopActive.description: Active tab with accent background and auto height (no fixed height, expands for description)

tabButtonTitle.description: Anton heading inside tab button, maps to 40px on desktop (text-header-5 = 3xl = 40px)

tabButtonDescription.description: Description text below title, only visible on active tab. General Sans (font-sans) by default.

contentPanelDesktop.description: Right column wrapper that vertically centers the content panel

contentPanelInner.description: The actual content panel with lighter accent background

tabsContainerDesktop.description: Inner blocks wrapper inside the content panel

mobileAccordionArea.description: Mobile-only accordion view. Shows all tabs as expandable accordion items.

mobileAccordionItemActive.description: When open, the item gets accent background

mobileAccordionHeader.classes: flex items-start gap-4 p-6 w-full text-left cursor-pointer
mobileAccordionHeader.description: Clickable header row with title and plus/minus icon

mobileAccordionTitle.description: Anton heading at 32px on mobile (text-header-6 = 2xl = 32px mobile)

mobileAccordionIcon.description: Plus/minus toggle icon (~18.67px). Use CSS lines like the Accordion block does.

mobileAccordionContent.description: Content area below header, only visible when open

innerBlockContent.description: Prose styling for inner block content within each tab panel

mobile.behavior: Tabs become accordion items. Each tab is a full-width rounded box. User can open multiple or no tabs. Plus/minus icon indicates open/close state. Content appears inline inside the open accordion item. The 'second title' (content panel heading) is hidden on mobile - only the accordion header title is visible. Description text appears below the title inside the accordion header when open.
mobile.layout: Single column, stacked accordion items with gap-4
mobile.themeHeading: Single column layout (stacked eyebrow, heading, description, buttons)

desktop.behavior: Two-column tab layout. Left column has vertical tab buttons (rounded-left with left/top/bottom border). Right column shows content panel with accent-lighter background. One tab is always selected. Active tab button has accent background and shows description text.
desktop.layout: Two equal columns side by side

desktopTabSelection.description: One tab must always be selected on desktop. Clicking a tab button selects it and shows its content panel. The active tab button gets bg-accent, shows the description, and loses its fixed height. The content panel updates to show the selected tab's inner blocks.
desktopTabSelection.defaultTab: First tab (index 0) is selected by default

mobileAccordionBehavior.description: On mobile, tabs work like accordions. Multiple tabs can be open simultaneously. All tabs can be closed (no requirement for one to always be open).
mobileAccordionBehavior.toggleBehavior: Clicking an accordion header toggles it open/closed

responsiveTransition.description: When resizing from mobile to desktop, if all accordion items are closed, the first tab should be automatically selected. This ensures the desktop constraint of 'one always selected' is maintained.
responsiveTransition.mobileToDesktop: If no accordion is open, select first tab
responsiveTransition.desktopToMobile: The currently selected tab's accordion should be open, others closed

editorBehavior.tabTitleSync: useEffect watches innerBlocks tabTitle attributes and syncs to parent tabsHeading array
editorBehavior.newTabAutoSelect: When a new tab is added, auto-select it in the editor
editorBehavior.tabContextProvider: TabsCurrent React context wraps inner blocks with currentTab index

accentColorPattern.description: The background color of tab items depends on the page accent color, following the same pattern as the Accordion block. The accent color is set at the page level via CSS custom properties (--accent-color, --accent-color-light, --accent-color-lighter) and applied using utility classes (bg-accent, bg-accent-lighter).

usage.activeTabButton: bg-accent (the main accent color, e.g., yellow #ffd24c, blue #47cbf2, orange #f26d45)
usage.contentPanel: bg-accent-lighter (the lighter shade, e.g., yellow-lighter #ffedb7, blue-lighter #b5eafa)
usage.mobileOpenAccordion: bg-accent (same as active tab button on desktop)

cssVariables.--accent-color: Main accent color (set per page/section via color classes like .yellow, .blue, .orange, etc.)
cssVariables.--accent-color-lighter: Lighter shade (used for content panel background)

accentColorPattern.noHardcodedColors: Never hardcode hex values. Always use bg-accent / bg-accent-lighter utilities so the color adapts to the page's accent color setting.

tabButton.ariaSelected: true for active tab, false for others
tabButton.ariaControls: ID of the corresponding tab panel
tabButton.tabindex: 0 for active tab, -1 for inactive tabs
tabButton.id: Generated unique ID for aria-labelledby on panels

tabPanel.ariaLabelledby: ID of the corresponding tab button
tabPanel.hidden: true for non-active panels (use hidden + inert attributes)

mobile.description: On mobile, ARIA switches from tablist pattern to standard button/region pattern since it's accordion behavior.

accordionHeader.ariaExpanded: true when open, false when closed
accordionHeader.ariaControls: ID of the accordion content panel

accordionContent.ariaLabelledby: ID of the accordion header button

accessibility.responsiveModeSwitch: On resize, switch ARIA roles between tablist mode (desktop) and button/region mode (mobile)

frontendJs.mobileBreakpoint: window.matchMedia('(max-width: 47.99rem)') to match md breakpoint at 48rem
features: Tab selection on desktop (click tab button to switch)
Accordion toggle on mobile (click to open/close)
ARIA attribute management (roles, aria-selected, aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-labelledby)
Keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down for vertical tabs, Home/End)
Mobile-to-desktop transition: if all accordions closed, select first tab
Desktop-to-mobile transition: open the currently selected tab's accordion
Hidden + inert on closed accordion content (mobile)
Plus/minus icon toggle animation on mobile

stateManagement.selectedTabIndex: Track which tab is selected (desktop mode)
stateManagement.openAccordions: Track which accordion items are open (mobile mode, Set or array of indices)
stateManagement.currentMode: Track current mode (desktop vs mobile) to handle transitions

parent.note: Use custom Appender component for adding new tab items

child.note: Tab content can contain any blocks

decisions.question: How should the tab buttons be shaped on desktop?
decisions.decision: Left-border-only with rounded-l-3xl (25px left corners). No right border, creating a pill that connects to the content panel edge.
decisions.rationale: Figma design 785:34910 shows border-l, border-t, border-b (no border-r) with rounded-bl-[25px] rounded-tl-[25px]. The tab buttons visually merge with the content panel on the right.

decisions.question: What color should the active tab button use?
decisions.decision: bg-accent (page accent color) for the active tab button background
decisions.rationale: Figma annotation on 785:34910 states 'Colour will change based on the pillar'. Desktop1 shows yellow (#ffd24c = accent for yellow pillar), Desktop2 shows blue (#47cbf2 = accent for blue pillar). This maps to bg-accent utility.

decisions.question: What color should the content panel use?
decisions.decision: bg-accent-lighter for the content panel background
decisions.rationale: Desktop1 panel is #ffedb7 (yellow-lighter), Desktop2 panel is #b5eafa (blue-lighter). These map to the --accent-color-lighter CSS variable, used via bg-accent-lighter utility.

decisions.question: How should tabs behave on mobile?
decisions.decision: Accordion pattern. Multiple can be open. All can be closed. Plus/minus icon toggle.
decisions.rationale: Spec states: 'On mobile it's going to work like accordions. On mobile the user can have multiple or no tabs (accordions) open.' Figma mobile design (785:33971) shows full-width rounded boxes with plus/minus icons.

decisions.question: What happens when transitioning from mobile to desktop with all tabs closed?
decisions.decision: Auto-select the first tab when switching from mobile (all closed) to desktop.
decisions.rationale: Spec states: 'When changing from mobile to desktop, if all tabs (accordions) are closed, the first one should be selected.'

decisions.question: Should the content panel heading (tab title repeated at top) be shown on mobile?
decisions.decision: Hidden on mobile. Only the accordion header title is visible.
decisions.rationale: Figma annotation on mobile design (785:33971) states: 'Second title (Tab title) is hidden on mobile, only main (this one) is visible'.

decisions.question: What heading size should the ThemeHeading use?
decisions.rationale: Figma shows font/size/5xl (56px) for the heading, which maps to text-header-3 (2rem mobile, 3xl = ~48px responsive). ThemeHeading with headingSize=3 uses the H3 heading tier. The two-column layout is evident in all desktop Figma designs (eyebrow+heading left, description+buttons right).

decisions.question: Should inactive tab buttons have a fixed height?
decisions.decision: Yes, inactive tab buttons have a fixed height of h-[140px] on desktop with vertically centered content. Active tab buttons have auto height to accommodate the description text.
decisions.rationale: Figma design context shows h-[140px] for inactive tab items and no fixed height for the active tab (which expands for description content).

decisions.question: What color should the mobile accordion items use when open?
decisions.rationale: Mobile Figma (785:33971) shows #f26d45 for the open accordion item, which is the orange accent main color (not lighter). This matches bg-accent.

decisions.question: Should the tab content panel title be duplicated from the tab button title?
decisions.decision: Yes, on desktop the content panel shows the active tab's title at the top as an H5-level heading (Anton, 40px). This is duplicated from the tab button. On mobile, this second title is hidden.
decisions.rationale: Both desktop Figma designs show the tab title repeated at the top of the content panel. Mobile annotation explicitly states the second title is hidden.

decisions.question: What font should the tab button title use on desktop vs mobile?
decisions.decision: Desktop: font-heading text-header-5 (Anton, font/size/3xl = 40px). Mobile: font-heading text-header-6 (Anton, font/size/2xl = 32px).
decisions.rationale: Desktop Figma uses Headline/H5 style (font/size/3xl = 40px). Mobile Figma uses Headline/H6 style (font/size/2xl = 32px).

decisions.question: Should the tab description appear on inactive tab buttons?
decisions.decision: No, the description text only appears on the active/selected tab button. Inactive buttons show only the title.
decisions.rationale: Figma designs show description text only in the active tab button (both desktop and mobile open accordion). Inactive buttons have fixed height with only the title visible.

decisions.question: How should the content inside the content panel be structured?
decisions.decision: The content panel contains inner blocks rendered as prose content. The tab content is free-form: it can include rich text, sub-sections with headings, bullet lists, and 'Learn More' link buttons with horizontal dividers between sections.
decisions.rationale: Figma designs show varied content including bold text, paragraphs, bullet lists, sub-headings (Headline/H7 = font/size/xl = 24px), and 'LEARN MORE' arrow links with divider lines between sections. The content panel annotation also states 'Ability to stylize text: bold, italic, bullet points, numbered lists, etc.' This is best handled by inner blocks.

decisions.question: Should a custom-variant for 'not-open' be added to states.css?
decisions.decision: No custom variant needed. The Accordion uses native <details> open/not-open CSS pseudo-classes. For Tabs, we use JS-managed classes (.is-open, .selected) and the existing 'open' custom variant from states.css for the mobile accordion items.
decisions.rationale: The Accordion uses <details> HTML element which has native open/not-open support. Tabs will use div-based elements with JS-managed state classes. The 'open' custom variant in states.css already supports .is-open class matching.

decisions.question: How should the tab description be handled as an attribute?
decisions.decision: Add tabDescription as an optional string attribute on the child block (takt/tabs-item). This text appears below the tab title in the tab button on both desktop (active only) and mobile accordion header (open only).
decisions.rationale: The Figma designs show a description text (e.g., 'Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.') that appears below the tab title in the active tab button. This is distinct from the inner block content. It needs to be a separate attribute to display in the parent's tab navigation area.

statesCss.description: No changes needed to states.css. The existing 'open' custom variant supports .is-open class which will be used for the mobile accordion open state.

cssRequirements.additionalNote: The tabDescription on mobile accordion items could use the open: variant for visibility toggling.

tabDescriptionSync.description: Similar to tabsHeading, the parent needs a tabsDescription array synced from child tabDescription attributes, so the parent can render descriptions in the tab buttons.

parentAttribute.name: tabsDescription
parentAttribute.description: Array of tab descriptions synced from child tabDescription attributes

tabDescriptionSync.syncLogic: Same useEffect pattern as tabsHeading sync - watch innerBlocks for tabDescription changes and update parent array

notes: Test mobile accordion behavior: open/close multiple items, verify plus/minus icon toggles
Test desktop tab selection: click tabs, verify content panel changes, verify accent color on active tab
Test responsive transition: open 0 accordions on mobile, resize to desktop, verify first tab is auto-selected
Test responsive transition: select tab 3 on desktop, resize to mobile, verify tab 3 accordion is open
Test keyboard navigation on desktop: ArrowUp/Down to move between tabs, Enter/Space to select, Home/End for first/last
Test all accent colors: yellow, blue, orange, green, purple, neon-green
Verify content panel uses bg-accent-lighter (NOT bg-accent) on desktop
Verify mobile open accordion uses bg-accent (NOT bg-accent-lighter)
Verify the second title is hidden on mobile (only accordion header title visible)
Verify ARIA attributes switch between tablist mode (desktop) and button/region mode (mobile)
